notable_pick_trades: Own two 1st-rounders (No. 2 overall, No. 16 via IND from Sauce Gardner trade). Two 2nd-rounders (No. 33 own, No. 44 via DAL from Quinnen Williams trade). Owe 3rd-round pick to PHI (Haason Reddick trade). Received comp picks in Rd 4 (No. 140) and Rd 5 (No. 179). Traded 6th-rounder to LV for Geno Smith + 7th. Have two 7th-rounders (No. 228, No. 242).
roster_context: Coming off a historically bad 3-14 season that included zero interceptions (an NFL first) and only 26 sacks (second-fewest in league). Traded Sauce Gardner and Quinnen Williams at midseason deadline for future draft capital. HC Aaron Glenn is taking over defensive play-calling and transitioning to a hybrid 3-4 front. The franchise lacks a long-term QB and is in full rebuild mode but must show improvement in 2026 to save Glenn's job. Aggressive defensive free agency has addressed safety, IDL, EDGE depth, and LB — but WR remains completely unaddressed beyond Garrett Wilson. Four picks in the top 44 provide enormous draft capital to reshape roster.

needs.explanation: The Jets recorded the second-fewest sacks in the NFL (26) in 2025 and traded Jermaine Johnson to the Titans. Will McDonald IV is a talented pass-rush specialist but a liability against the run and not a starter in Glenn's base 3-4. Signings of Ossai and Enagbare are solid rotational pieces, but neither is an elite difference-maker. The defense needs a true closer — a game-wrecking edge rusher to anchor the front. Arvell Reese (Ohio State) is the overwhelming consensus pick at No. 2 overall.
needs.current_starter: Joseph Ossai (FA signing), Kingsley Enagbare (FA signing) as base EDGE/OLBs; Will McDonald IV as sub-package pass rusher
needs.depth: McDonald is the best pure pass rusher but can't play base downs. Ossai and Enagbare are mid-tier starters. Tyler Baron (2025 draft pick) is a developmental piece.

needs.explanation: The Jets did not have a single receiver reach 400 receiving yards in 2025. Garrett Wilson led the team with just 395 yards before a knee injury ended his season after 7 games. After Wilson, the depth chart is barren: Adonai Mitchell (acquired in Gardner trade, showed flashes), Isaiah Williams (primarily a returner), Arian Smith, and John Metchie III. No WR has been added in free agency. This is the clear-cut No. 1 offensive need and the team is expected to address it at No. 16 overall.
needs.current_starter: Garrett Wilson (WR1, returning from knee injury), Adonai Mitchell (WR2)
needs.depth: Isaiah Williams, Arian Smith, Quentin Skinner — arguably the weakest WR depth in the NFL beyond Wilson

needs.explanation: Geno Smith (age 35) was acquired via trade from Las Vegas as a bridge quarterback at minimal cost (~$3.3M). Justin Fields is expected to be released (post-June 1 cut likely) after a disastrous 2025 season. This is not a long-term solution — Smith has had two consecutive bad seasons. The 2026 QB class is considered weak beyond Fernando Mendoza (No. 1 pick), so the Jets may take a Day 2 QB or wait for the 2027 class where they hold three 1st-round picks. Still a franchise-altering need.
needs.depth: Justin Fields (expected to be cut), Brady Cook, Shane Buechele — no viable long-term answer on roster
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2 (No. 33 or 44) or trade back into Round 1. Could also wait for 2027 class.

needs.explanation: The Sauce Gardner trade left a massive void. Brandon Stephens proved he's a quality CB2 but not a true CB1 (PFF grade 67, ranked 40th). Azareye'h Thomas (2025 3rd-rounder) showed promise but had rookie growing pains. Jarvis Brownlee Jr. emerged as a solid slot corner. The Jets signed Nahshon Wright (NFL leader in takeaways with 5 INTs in 2025), which helps, but there's no true shutdown CB1 on the roster. For a team that recorded zero interceptions in 2025, this remains a significant need.
needs.current_starter: Brandon Stephens (CB1), Azareye'h Thomas (CB2), Jarvis Brownlee Jr. (slot), Nahshon Wright (FA signing)
needs.depth: Qwan'tez Stiggers provides additional depth. Wright signing helps but is on a 1-year prove-it deal.

needs.explanation: The acquisition of Minkah Fitzpatrick (3-year, $40M) via trade from Miami was a home run. He's still playing at a high level (81.8 PFF grade in 2025) and brings leadership and ball-hawking ability. Andre Cisco was re-signed on a 1-year deal and Dane Belton was added for depth. The position is markedly improved from 2025 but Fitzpatrick is 29 and Cisco is on a prove-it deal. A young developmental safety could be targeted on Day 2 or 3.
needs.current_starter: Minkah Fitzpatrick (FS, trade from MIA), Andre Cisco (SS, re-signed)
needs.depth: Dane Belton (FA signing), Malachi Moore — much improved but long-term second safety spot is unsettled

needs.explanation: Both starting guards from 2025 departed in free agency: John Simpson (signed with BAL) and Alijah Vera-Tucker (signed with NE). The Jets signed Dylan Parham from the Raiders as the new starting LG, and Joe Tippmann remains at RG after a breakout season. Josh Myers is the starting center on a team-friendly extension. The starting 5 is set but OL depth is thin — only Max Mitchell (re-signed) and Xavier Newman provide backup. One injury could expose the unit.
needs.current_starter: Dylan Parham (LG, FA signing), Joe Tippmann (RG), Josh Myers (C)
needs.depth: Max Mitchell (swing OT/G), Xavier Newman (G/C), Chukwuma Okorafor (re-signed, backup OT) — thin but functional

needs.explanation: Demario Davis (37, 2-year/$22M) was signed to be the MIKE linebacker and defensive leader with the green dot. Jamien Sherwood enters year 3 but regressed significantly in 2025 after C.J. Mosley's departure. Mykal Walker was re-signed as quality depth. Davis addresses the immediate need but is 37 — his coverage metrics declined last year. A young LB to develop behind Davis and push Sherwood would be valuable.
needs.depth: Mykal Walker (re-signed), Marcelino McCrary-Ball, Kiko Mauigoa — adequate for now

needs.explanation: Significantly addressed via trade and free agency. T'Vondre Sweat (acquired from TEN for Jermaine Johnson) is a 366-pound nose tackle who anchors Glenn's 3-4 front. David Onyemata (33, FA signing) adds another 310-pound run stuffer. Harrison Phillips and Jowon Briggs round out a deep rotation. This was a critical need entering the offseason but has been adequately addressed. A Day 3 developmental piece could add long-term value.
needs.current_starter: T'Vondre Sweat (NT, 0-tech), Harrison Phillips (4i), David Onyemata (4i)
needs.depth: Jowon Briggs provides quality rotation depth — DT room is now a strength

free_agency_impact: Traded for QB Geno Smith from LV (6th-round pick sent, received Smith + 7th) — bridge QB, does NOT solve long-term QB need
Traded for S Minkah Fitzpatrick from MIA (7th-round pick sent) + signed 3-year/$40M extension — major S upgrade
Signed EDGE Joseph Ossai (3-year/$34.5M, $22.5M guaranteed) — addresses EDGE depth but not elite pass rusher
Signed EDGE Kingsley Enagbare (1-year/$10M) — rotational EDGE/force player in 3-4
Signed LB Demario Davis (2-year/$22M, $15M guaranteed) — leadership + MIKE LB upgrade
Signed DT David Onyemata (FA from ATL) — run-stuffing 4i-tech for 3-4 front
Traded for DT T'Vondre Sweat from TEN (sent Jermaine Johnson) — anchoring nose tackle for 3-4 scheme transition
Signed CB Nahshon Wright (1-year/$5.5M) — led NFL in takeaways in 2025 (5 INTs), addresses historic INT drought
Re-signed S Andre Cisco (1-year/$5.25M) — retains starting SS
Signed S Dane Belton (1-year/$4M, up to $6M) — safety depth
Signed IOL Dylan Parham (FA from LV) — replaces departed John Simpson at LG
Re-signed LB Mykal Walker (1-year) — LB depth
Re-signed OT Max Mitchell (1-year), OT Chukwuma Okorafor (1-year) — OL depth
Franchise tagged RB Breece Hall ($14.293M) — retains top RB
Lost G John Simpson to BAL (3-year/$30M) — departed starting LG
Lost G Alijah Vera-Tucker to NE — departed guard
Lost K Nick Folk to ATL (2-year/$9M) — kicker now unresolved
Lost DE Michael Clemons to IND (3-year/$17.5M) — rotational DL departed
Justin Fields expected to be released (post-June 1 cut) — $22M dead cap, clears roster spot
WR room completely unaddressed in free agency — remains critical draft need
scheme_fit_notes: Aaron Glenn is transitioning to a hybrid 3-4 front after firing DC Steve Wilks and taking over play-calling. Glenn ran a one-gap, Eagle-front 3-4 in Detroit with heavy use of odd fronts but still leans on even-front principles in sub-packages. This means the Jets need versatile EDGE players who can play as 3-4 OLBs (force players against the run) AND rush the passer from a two-point stance. A true 0-tech nose tackle (Sweat) anchors the front. Off-ball LBs must be smart, physical, and able to stack-and-shed in a react-attack scheme. On offense, new OC Frank Reich runs an Erhardt-Perkins system that favors big pocket passers, play-action, and screen concepts — WR versatility and route-running matter more than pure speed.
